name: Salesforce

hero.heading: Gapflow and Salesforce

documentation.title: Getting Started with Salesforce
documentation.description: Learn how to connect your Salesforce account and set up your first automation workflow.

documentation.title: Advanced Salesforce Operations
documentation.description: Discover advanced techniques for bulk operations, custom objects, and complex field mappings.

documentation.title: Salesforce API Best Practices

tutorials.title: Build Your First Salesforce Automation
tutorials.description: Step-by-step tutorial on creating a simple lead routing automation that enriches leads with external data, scores them based on custom criteria, and automatically assigns them to the right sales rep.

tutorials.title: Sync Salesforce with Your Data Warehouse
tutorials.description: Learn how to set up automated data pipelines that sync your Salesforce data to BigQuery or Snowflake, enabling advanced analytics and business intelligence reporting.

tutorials.title: AI-Powered Case Management
tutorials.description: Discover how to leverage AI to automatically categorize, prioritize, and route support cases, while generating intelligent summaries for your support team.

faqs.question: How do I authenticate my Salesforce account?
faqs.answer: You can authenticate using OAuth 2.0. Simply click the 'Connect' button in the integration settings, and you'll be redirected to Salesforce to authorize Gapflow. We support both production and sandbox environments.

faqs.question: What are the API rate limits?
faqs.answer: Salesforce enforces API limits based on your license type. Gapflow automatically handles rate limiting and implements exponential backoff. We recommend using bulk operations for large data sets to optimize API usage.

faqs.question: Can I sync custom objects?
faqs.answer: Yes! Gapflow fully supports Salesforce custom objects. You can trigger workflows based on custom object changes and perform CRUD operations on any custom object in your org.

faqs.question: How do I handle field-level security?
faqs.answer: Gapflow respects Salesforce field-level security settings. Ensure the connected user has appropriate permissions to read and write the fields used in your workflows.

faqs.question: Can I test automations in a sandbox?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. You can connect both production and sandbox environments. We recommend testing all workflows in a sandbox before deploying to production.

faqs.question: What happens if my workflow fails?
faqs.answer: Gapflow provides detailed error logs and automatic retry mechanisms. You can configure alerts to notify you via email or Slack when workflows fail, and easily debug issues using our execution history.

limits.title: API Rate Limits
limits.description: Salesforce enforces API limits based on your license type. Use bulk operations for large data sets and monitor your API usage in the Salesforce Setup menu.

limits.title: Field-Level Security
limits.description: Ensure the connected user has appropriate field-level and object-level permissions. Missing permissions will result in workflow failures.

limits.title: Bulk Operations
limits.description: For operations involving more than 200 records, use Salesforce Bulk API. Gapflow automatically switches to bulk mode when appropriate.

limits.title: Governor Limits
limits.description: Be aware of Salesforce governor limits including SOQL queries, DML statements, and CPU time. Design workflows to minimize resource consumption.
